name: Satyavan
description: Satyavan is a central figure in a poignant tale of love, devotion, and the triumph over death in Hindu mythology, particularly featured in the epic Mahabharata. Born to King Dyumatsena, who had lost his kingdom and eyesight, Satyavan grew up in the forest, embodying virtue, strength, and wisdom. He won the heart of Princess Savitri, despite a prophecy foretelling his death within a year. Savitri, undeterred by the grim prediction, chose to marry him, fully aware of his impending demise. As the prophecy neared fulfillment, Savitri maintained unwavering devotion, accompanying Satyavan into the forest daily. On the fateful day, Yama, the god of death, arrived to claim Satyavan's soul. Savitri, through her unwavering love, intelligence, and devotion, engaged Yama in a profound philosophical discourse. Impressed by her virtues, Yama granted her boons, each carefully worded to ensure Satyavan's life was restored. Through her wit and unwavering love, Savitri ultimately outsmarted Yama, securing Satyavan's return to life and restoring his father's kingdom and eyesight. Satyavan represents unwavering virtue, and the power of true love and devotion to overcome even death. His story is a powerful testament to the strength of character and the ability of a devoted partner to alter destiny. He embodies the ideal of a righteous and virtuous man, blessed by the unwavering love of Savitri.
subtitle: Love's triumph over death.
achievements: Living a virtuous life despite hardship
Playing a role in restoring his father's kingdom

keyMyths.title: The Prophecy of Death
keyMyths.description: Narada foretells Satyavan's death within a year of his marriage to Savitri. Savitri, aware of this, chooses to marry him anyway, demonstrating her unwavering commitment.

keyMyths.title: Savitri's Devotion
keyMyths.description: As the predicted day approaches, Savitri intensifies her devotion and accompanies Satyavan into the forest. She fasts and prays, preparing herself for the inevitable encounter with Yama.

keyMyths.title: Savitri Outwits Yama
keyMyths.description: Savitri, through her wisdom and devotion, engages Yama in a philosophical debate and secures boons that indirectly restore Satyavan's life and his father's kingdom.

sources.author: Vyasa
sources.description: The primary source for the story of Satyavan and Savitri.
sources.text: The primary source for the story of Satyavan and Savitri.
